title: hieracium rupicola subsp. squarrosulum
intro: Hieracium rupicola subsp. squarrosulum, also known as Squarrose Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hieracium rupicola subsp. squarrosulum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
